stories.title: To heal itself, the UK must face up to why it voted to leave the EU | Letters
stories.summary: We are watching Britain lose itself in imperial nostalgia and self-delusion, writes Prof Berthold Schoene. And Ian Arnott remembers a warning to Brexiters Michael Gove and Boris Johnson This kind of article annoys the hell out of me ( Boats, bankers and borders: five symbols that sum up Brexit a decade on, 20 June ). We all know that the leave campaign was built on lies and manipulation. We are yet to see a list of Brexit benefits. Or, indeed, a list of names of the very few who did actually ben

stories.title: Erdoğan launches crackdown ahead of NATO summit
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Turkish authorities have carried out hundreds of arrests, banned protests and curbed media freedoms ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara, with campaigners accusing the government of using the gathering as a pretext for a clampdown on civil society. On Tuesday, Turkish police detained 225 people including academics, teachers and activists on suspicion of links to militant groups like the Islamic State — among them a 79-year-old environmental campaigner . The city of Ankara has a

stories.title: Brexit Britain and the roots of its discontent | Letters
stories.summary: Readers respond to an article by Rafael Behr in which he reflects on the ‘curse of Brexit’ and the reasons for Keir Starmer’s fall Rafael Behr argues that Brexit created a politics poisoned by nationalism and that the real challenge facing Labour is “a battle to reclaim patriotism” ( Keir Starmer couldn’t beat the curse of Brexit – a politics poisoned by nationalism, 24 June ). Yet this framework risks reducing Britain’s political crisis to a dispute over competing versions o

stories.title: 'Operation Purgatory': Magyar moves to demolish Orban system
stories.summary: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former PM Viktor Orban. The focus is on fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

stories.title: Germany joins push to delay EU methane rules
stories.summary: LUXEMBOURG — Berlin will seek to delay the rollout of key European Union rules regulating emissions of the potent greenhouse gas methane, joining a growing coalition of EU member countries arguing the regulation could jeopardize the bloc’s supply of oil and gas. It follows sustained lobbying against the rules by the United States government and the fossil fuel sector. “We urgently need a reworking of the methane regulation,” Katherina Reiche, Germany’s minister of economi

stories.title: UK June heat record broken again, France postpones Pride and Poland warns of wildfires as heatwave grips Europe – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here: UK June heat record broken for third day in a row Fourth child dies in France as brutal heatwave forecast to shift east Over in the UK, firefighters are still trying to bring a large wildfire in Derbyshire under control. The blaze, which has burned over 500 square metres of moorland and woodland on Tintwistle Moor, near Glossop, broke out on Wednesday evening, with fire crews from Manchester and Derbyshire deploying a water-dropping

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
stories.summary: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues . In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared

stories.title: Csiszár Jenő és Orbán Viktor unokatestvére is a visszahívott külképviseleti vezetők között
stories.summary: A Telex információi szerint az SZFE átalakítását levezénylő Szarka Gábor, továbbá a londoni és a washingtoni magyar nagykövet megbízatása is véget ért.

stories.title: Új főispánokat nevezett ki Magyar Péter hét vármegye élére
stories.summary: Középtávon megszűnnek majd a vármegyék, a kormányhivataloktól is több hatáskör elkerül.

stories.title: Az Orbán-rendszer a lelkek megrontására ment rá, de kudarcot vallottak
stories.summary: A Fidesz melegellenessége a fiatalok elvesztéséhez és a választási vereséghez is hozzájárulhatott. A melegházasság és az örökbefogadás támogatottsága történelmi csúcson: de bevállalja a Tisza a jogegyenlőséget?

stories.title: Szombaton megszűnik a közmédia jelenlegi vezetőinek megbízatása
stories.summary: Távozik Koltay András, a Médiatanács elnöke és a testület tagjai, Altorjai Anita, a Duna Médiaszolgáltató Nonprofit Zrt. vezetője és Papp Dániel, az MTVA vezérigazgatója.

stories.title: Trump gyors és olcsó megoldást ígért egy drága és bonyolult problémára. Meg is lett az eredménye
stories.summary: A Lincoln-emlékműnél lévő tükörmedence Washington egyik jelképe. A felújítás után a kékre festett medence előbb zöldre változott az elburjánzó algáktól, aztán elkezdett feljönni a frissen lerakott festék, a tisztítóberendezések meghibásodtak, és még egy döglött kacsát is fotóztak a vízben. Trumpnak nem kellett sok, hogy megtalálja a valódi felelősöket, és tényleg történt vandalizmus is a medencénél.

stories.title: Szenegál tönkreverte a szinte végig emberhátrányban játszó Irakot, és még van esélye továbbjutni
stories.summary: Hamar vezetést szereztek, aztán hamar kerültek emberelőnybe is, a második félidőben pedig feljebb tudtak kapcsolni, és lőttek még négyet a fáradó irakiaknak. A vége 5-0 lett.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Ha belépünk az eurózónába, egy kormány sem teheti majd meg azt, amit Orbán Viktor tett
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint az Orbán-kormány olyan volt, mint egy apuka, aki eljátssza a gyerek taníttatására félretett pénzt, ettől viszont megvéd az, ha bevezetjük az eurót.

stories.title: Az elmúlt évek változásait a kutatóknál már csak a kutatástámogatók szenvedték meg jobban
stories.summary: A Telexen megjelent véleménycikkében Hillier Dániel arról írt, hogy a magyar tudomány legnagyobb problémája nem a pénzhiány, hanem az intézményi bénultság: hiányzik az az adminisztratív háttér, amely enyhíthetné a kutatókra nehezedő bürokratikus terhelést. Szécsi Dorottya válaszcikkében megoldási javaslatokat tett erre a problémára: a modern vezetőképzés meghonosítását és az adminisztratív kutatássegítő szakemberek megbecsültségének növelését. Sorozatunk zárórészében Tamás Veronika mutat be egy

stories.title: Közel 150 év után politikai napilap nélkül marad Magyarország
stories.summary: A Népszava a Mediaworks keménykedésének esett áldozatul, a Magyar Nemzetet pedig augusztustól csak hetente jelenteti meg a kiadó. A kiegyezés óta először nem lesz tartósan nyomatott politikai napilap.

stories.title: Keressük a Balaton legolcsóbb és legdrágább lángosát
stories.summary: Egy jó lángosozás a nyár kihagyhatatlan része. Mi most az olvasóink segítségét kérjük, hogy tisztábban lássunk a magyar lángospiacon.

stories.title: Térképen, ahogy a hőkupola egyre inkább rátelepszik Magyarországra
stories.summary: Egyre durvul a helyzet, az extrém hőség jövő hét közepéig nem enged a szorításból. 40 fok fölötti hőmérsékletekre számítson mindenki.

stories.title: A baglyok nem tudják, mit kell tenni, amikor 130-cal érkezik egy fényes, egytonnás valami
stories.summary: A táj feldarabolódásában jelentős szerepet játszanak az egyre-másra épülő utak, amikben Magyarország élen jár. Elütött baglyok tömegei jelzik, hogy át kellett volna gondolni a gyorsforgalmi utak építését.

stories.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
stories.summary: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

stories.title: Seal at Hungarian zoo brushes teeth
stories.summary: A seal at Nyíregyháza Zoo in Hungary brushes its teeth in the Arctic Ice World exhibit, showcasing daily care and animal enrichment activities.

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

stories.title: Mahmood outlines safe immigration routes plan to win over Labour left
stories.summary: Home secretary speeds up major part of bill governing asylum and refugees as new prime minister set to take over Shabana Mahmood will seek to shore up support for her controversial immigration bill on the progressive left of Labour, as she sets out plans to speed up the opening of new safe and legal routes that will permit thousands of refugees to come to the UK. The home secretary, who is the leading contender to stay in her job if Andy Burnham becomes prime minister, will next week introduce t

stories.title: Trump threatens additional tariffs on European countries using digital service taxes
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday threatened a 100 percent tariff on European countries that impose digital service taxes on American corporations. In a  post to social media , the president said “numerous European countries“ are discussing or “close to actually doing this.” “Please let this statement serve to represent that any Country that imposes such a Tax will immediately be met with a 100% TARIFF on any and all Goods sent to the United States of America,” Trump said. “This TARIFF

stories.title: To defend against hybrid attacks, governments should team up with the private sector
stories.summary: Elisabeth Braw is a senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, author of the award-winning “Goodbye Globalization” and a regular columnist for POLITICO. Her new book, “ The Undersea War ,” is out later this year. From cyberattacks to arson and drone surveillance, these days European companies are being targeted by all manner of nefarious attacks. These businesses are crucial to the daily functioning of our nations; indeed, that may be the reason they’re being targeted. And yet, in most countries, bu

stories.title: Trump blames Iran for drone strike on cargo ship in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US president calls it ‘foolish violation’ of ceasefire agreement after drone damaged ship’s upper deck US politics live – latest updates Donald Trump on Friday blamed Iran for carrying out a drone strike on a cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz, calling it a “foolish violation” of the ceasefire agreement with the US. One drone damaged the upper deck of the ship but the ship was able to proceed, the US president said. The US shot down three other drones aimed at the ship, he said. Continue reading

stories.title: Home secretary sought to restrict minister’s access to papers as she calls for his sacking
stories.summary: Row between Shabana Mahmood and Mike Tapp broke out after he wrote unauthorised article about immigration A rift between Keir Starmer and Shabana Mahmood has deepened after it emerged the home secretary wanted to deny the migration minister, Mike Tapp, access to sensitive documents without her approval as she called for him to be sacked. Downing Street said Tapp – who has been a loyal supporter of Keir Starmer – remained a minister but that the prime minister was taking advice on whether he brok

stories.title: JD Vance says ‘violence will be met with violence’ after strikes on Iran – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. US says it struck Iran targets after attack on cargo ship on the strait of Hormuz Sign up for the Breaking News US email Hayes went on: As national security adviser to the president of the United States, Mr Bolton had access to and was responsible for safeguarding the most sensitive national defense information, including classified material. Mr Bolton knew how to handle classified information, where it should be stored, how it should be stored, and with whom he cou

stories.title: Iran war: US military hits Iran over cargo vessel attack
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has accused Iran of carrying out a drone attack on a cargo vessel on Thursday and violating the US-Iran deal. In response, the US military has launched fresh strikes on Iran. DW has more.

stories.title: Britain’s next leader faces a big call on North Sea drilling. Donald Trump is watching.
stories.summary: LONDON — Labour MPs and climate campaigners are abandoning resistance to a controversial new North Sea gas field ahead of Andy Burnham’s expected entry into Downing Street next month — but they say they’ll carry on fighting over a second, even more contentious, oil field.   Burnham, who is on a path to becoming Labour leader and U.K. prime minister within weeks, is under pressure from the party’s union backers to approve Jackdaw, a major new gas development 150 miles east of Aberdeen i

stories.title: EU Commission proposes excluding fighting-age men from Ukraine refugee scheme
stories.summary: The European Commission on Friday proposed extending temporary protection status for individuals fleeing the war in Ukraine until March 2028 — but excluding newly arrived men of fighting age. The temporary protection scheme offers more than 4 million people who fled Ukraine the right to live, work and study in EU countries. The scheme has been extended several times since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion. Barring another extension, it will expire in March 2027. Under the Commissio

stories.title: VW reportedly looks to ax 100,000 jobs amid financial woes
stories.summary: Germany’s biggest automaker Volkswagen plans to cut about 15 percent of jobs from its workforce of more than 650,000, according to a Friday report from Manager Magazin business magazine. VW had previously announced it would cut about 50,000 jobs by 2030. Friday’s report says CEO Oliver Blume intends to double that figure, close four factories and spin off the VW brand. The automotive giant has been struggling financially, reporting a 14.3 percent drop in operating profit in the first quart

stories.title: Why Europe’s heat waves are still so deadly
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Once the heat dissipates, Europe will start counting its dead.  The extraordinary temperatures sweeping the western part of the continent this week will have killed hundreds, likely thousands, more than two decades after the deadly summer of 2003 alerted governments to the dangers of extreme heat.  While a first estimate of the death toll will take weeks, Spanish researchers are already attributing more than 210 fatalities between Sunday and Wednesday to the heat.

stories.title: Here we Joh again? The spectre of Bjelke-Petersen still looms large over Queensland
stories.summary: The LNP’s unapologetic moves on Indigenous issues, crime, transgender healthcare and police-led suppression of protests are a throwback to days many thought had come and gone Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A sign stopped Aunty Sandra King in her tracks. The elder of the Yagara, Quandamooka and Bundjalung people, now in her 70s, spoke at a protest last month against plans to build an Olympic stadium in the heart of Brisbane’s Victoria Park. In the crowd a man held al

stories.title: US says it struck Iran targets after attack on cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Strikes against military facilities were in response to drone attack a day earlier on a cargo vessel US strikes Iran – latest updates The US has struck Iran in a tit-for-tat response to a drone strike on a cargo ship, as the ceasefire between the US and Iran that reopened the strait of Hormuz undergoes its greatest test yet. The US strikes targeted multiple missile and drone facilities in Iran near the strait of Hormuz and on Qeshm Island on Friday in what appeared to be a limited strike meant t

stories.title: Trump attacks Democrats as ‘godless communists’ in test of midterms message
stories.summary: President seizes on wins by Mamdani-backed candidates to warn darkly of threat to ‘traditional American way of life’ Donald Trump has previewed a Republican strategy for the midterm elections, seizing on a progressive sweep in New York to portray Democrats as “godless communists” who pose an existential threat to the nation. The US president, who was a child during the “red scare”, seized on wins by democratic socialists backed by the mayor of New York City, Zohran Mamdani , to stoke fears that

stories.title: Gavin Newsom urges a national 'billionaires' tax' while fighting one in California
stories.summary: California governor calls for national tax on super-wealthy and suggests the US should own a stake in AI companies US politics live – latest updates California’s governor, Gavin Newsom , called for a national “billionaires tax” on Friday as he fights a ballot measure targeting the ultra-wealthy in his home state. Newsom, who is expected to run for president in 2028, published his proposal the day after California officials certified a ballot measure to levy a one-time 5% tax on residents worth m

stories.title: Polygamous sect leader guilty of abuse after girls found in trailer on highway
stories.summary: Self-proclaimed prophet Samuel Bateman already serving 50-year prison sentence over child sexual abuse A polygamous sect leader already serving a 50-year federal prison sentence for orchestrating sex involving children was convicted Friday on state child abuse charges after girls were found in an unventilated trailer he was hauling through Arizona . Someone alerted authorities about the trailer in 2022 after seeing small fingers reaching through gaps in the doors. Police stopped Samuel Bateman’s

stories.title: Montenegro arrests Iran-linked hacker wanted by US
stories.summary: The FBI was helping the Montenegrin police detain an Iranian-Turkish national whose cyberattacks on US infrastructure allegedly caused billions in damages.

stories.title: Venezuela updates earthquake toll: 920 dead, 3,360 injured
stories.summary: National Assembly president Jorge Rodríguez updated the figure on Friday. The UN reports over 50,000 missing. Spain urges its citizens in Venezuela to contact consular emergency services.

stories.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building
stories.summary: Police and emergency crews sealed off the area around Beijing's tallest skyscraper after a small plane crash damaged the building. Images from the scene showed a large hole in the upper floors of the 528-metre CITIC Tower, while witnesses reported seeing aircraft debris at the base of the building.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.title: US conducts strikes on Iran after attack on cargo ship
stories.summary: US Central Command says it has struck missile and drone storage facilities and coastal radar positions.

stories.title: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
stories.summary: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

stories.title: Adapting to the heat: four ideas from European cities
stories.summary: From checking on older neighbours to greening spaces, some cities are stepping up efforts to keep people safe Europe live – latest updates Extreme heat has seared Europe this week, with the UK smashing its top heat record for June for three days in a row, and France sweltering through its hottest day and night on record. As fossil fuel pollution bakes the planet, making heatwaves hotter and longer , some places are adapting better than others. What have European cities done to stay safe when it

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: 589 confirmed dead so far as international rescue teams arrive – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here China’s president Xi Jinping said Beijing was ready to provide Venezuela with “disaster relief and reconstruction” assistance. Xi sent a message of condolence to Venezuela’s interim president Delcy Rodríguez today, according to Chinese state news agency Xinhua. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘We feel like the peasants’: women and low-income families bear brunt of heatwave
stories.summary: As temperatures soar across Europe, cities are struggling to adapt, further exacerbating socioeconomic divisions The heatwave afflicting western Europe is the worst ever , with the combination of heat and humidity fuelled by the climate crisis making scores of cities feel unliveable. While for some the adverse impacts amount to disturbed sleep and sticky days in the home office, low-income families are often worse affected by cities’ lack of adequate adaptation measures, with women at the sharp

stories.title: Massive wins and the promise of change – but that’s where the Starmer-Albanese similarities end
stories.summary: While Anthony Albanese calls ousted British PM Keir Starmer his mate, the pair share very different political fortunes Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese channelled Paul Keating as he spoke to government MPs in Canberra this week, reflecting on the tough work of reform. Keating, Albanese said, considered Labor to be a bit like a bicycle. “It only stands up when you pedal,” the party grandee once warned, suggesting that without ideas, direction or moment

stories.title: VW plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs and shut plants, report says
stories.summary: German firm reportedly considering doubling previously announced staff reductions amid Chinese competition Business live – latest updates Germany’s Volkswagen is to cut up to 100,000 jobs and reduce and eventually stop production at some plants, according to reports. The company has refused to comment on reports of a management presentation at a board meeting outlining dramatic cost cutting, but if it goes ahead it would mean Volkswagen doubling previously announced staff reductions. Continue re

stories.title: Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on
stories.summary: Worst incident of its kind in country for more than 125 years leaves many searching for family members – and pleading for international help Venezuela death toll doubles as interim president vows to save ‘as many people as possible’ Nearly all of Ligia Level’s family lived in a trio of apartment blocks along Hotel Avenue, a seafront sweep of palm-specked resorts and high-rise condos along Venezuela’s Caribbean coast. When a powerful “doublet” of earthquakes jolted the region on Wednesday afterno

stories.title: Carney announces contest to revamp uninhabitable Canadian PM residence
stories.summary: Various issues – including a rodent infestation and mould – have left the historic, sprawling Ottawa estate empty 10 Downing Street has two things: mice and a chief mouser . For more than a decade, an officially recognized feline has kept rodent infestation in the British prime minister’s residence to a minimum. Over a similar period, the official residence of Canada’s prime minister has seen an unchecked explosion of rodents. Continue reading...

stories.title: Fourth toddler dies in France as Europe’s brutal heatwave forecast to shift east
stories.summary: Scientists say hot spell is worst ever, with nearly half of region’s 850 largest cities facing unprecedented heat stress The number of deaths in France linked to the heatwave has climbed to four toddlers and more than 55 drownings, as the brutally hot conditions sweeping Europe were forecast to shift east, choking 150 million people under 35C (95F) temperatures. Scientists said the heatwave was the most severe and widespread ever, leaving nearly half of the region’s 850 largest cities grappling

stories.title: Andy Burnham says he will give 15% of his MP’s pay to local causes
stories.summary: New member for Makerfield and expected next prime minister joins other MPs who donate some or all of their pay Andy Burnham has said he will be donating 15% of his MP’s salary to local causes in his constituency of Makerfield. An MP’s salary currently stands at £98,599 and a number of MPs donate all or part of their salary to charities and causes in the areas they represent. Continue reading...

stories.title: Now we know how much tax King Charles pays, and it is very little
stories.summary: The monarch’s declaration does not tell us much, except that his bill is lower than for people with much smaller fortunes The veil of secrecy that surrounds the royal finances was nudged aside a little on Thursday to allow the release of a new piece of information. We learned for the first time how much the king’s annual tax bill comes to. This was not a full tax return. It was a two-sentence declaration, stating his tax payable amounted to £12.9m in 2024-25, and a slightly smaller sum the year

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: The earthquake in Venezuela, a brutal heatwave in Europe, the resignation of Keir Starmer and the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Europeans voice concerns amid sweltering heatwave - video
stories.summary: European residents and tourists voiced their concerns over soaring temperatures as the continent continues to grapple with 'the most severe and widespread' heatwave ever. In summer 2022, more than 60,000 people died due to heat in Europe, and the current heatwave is already disrupting lives and livelihoods European heatwave is worst ever and impossible without climate crisis, scientists say Continue reading...

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll reaches 920 as interim president vows to save ‘as many as possible’
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez says foreign rescue teams are arriving as anger grows at official response and limited resources Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save “as many people as possible” as the official death toll from the country’s worst earthquake in more than a century almost doubled, but frustration was growing at the perceived sluggishness of the government’s response. Rodríguez’s

stories.title: Utah under historic ‘red flag’ weather warning amid dangerous wildfires
stories.summary: Governor also announced state of emergency and banned personal fireworks in state until after Fourth of July Salt Lake City’s National Weather Service declared a “particularly dangerous situation red flag warning”, the first in the service’s history, according to a social media post. “THIS IS EXTREMELY RARE … Wind gusts of 40-50+ mph, combined with hot temperatures and extremely dry air, could cause explosive wildfire growth. Any new fire that starts could spread rapidly and become difficult to

stories.title: Tracing one delicious snack around the Mediterranean showed me that modern borders are absurd | Federico De Blasi
stories.summary: Migration and cultural exchange have always been the norm between coastal European and African nations. We should celebrate this shared history We are used to mapping the world by continents, dividing the globe into rigid geopolitical blocks. But to understand the complex reality behind each border, we are better off using a different, edible kind of cartography. For most of human existence, the Mediterranean has existed as an intercultural entity in its own right, where peoples and languages fr

stories.title: South Korea to train half a million military personnel to become ‘drone warriors’
stories.summary: All branches of the military will be taught how to use technology that has become a ‘game changer on the battlefield’, says defence minister All of South Korea’s military forces will be trained as drone operators in a sweeping overhaul of its warfare strategy, the defence minister has said. “All soldiers should be able to use drones like a second personal firearm,” Ahn Gyu-back, who heads the defence ministry in Seoul, said on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Outrage as woman jailed for three years after criticising Somali government online
stories.summary: Sentencing of 27-year-old Sadia Moalim Ali condemned by former president and prime ministers as well as rights groups A rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention and provoked outrage in the country. Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old nursing graduate, was originally charged with insulting government institutions and incitement to commit a crime, but convicted only of the former. Her

stories.title: How the Iran war impacts Abu Dhabi's AI strategy
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi's ambitions to turn the UAE into a global hub for digital infrastructure and AI, dubbed "UAE AI Strategy 2031," face pressure after the war with Iran. But the UAE is also known for its business resilience.

stories.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
stories.summary: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

stories.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari set for Bayern Munich
stories.summary: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, is set to join Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

stories.title: Fact check: throwing cold water on heat wave disinformation
stories.summary: An outdated air-conditioning law, a viral health myth about sleeping with the fan on and the age-old climate change hoax allegation: DW checked what's spreading in Europe's heat wave.

stories.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
stories.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

stories.title: US, Lebanon, Israel sign framework agreement
stories.summary: US Secretary ‌of ⁠State ⁠Marco ​Rubio has announced ​a trilateral ​framework ‌agreement. Lebanese officials began direct talks with Israel in Washington in April.

stories.title: Trump foe John Bolton pleads guilty in classified docs case
stories.summary: Bolton faces as many as five years in prison. He had earlier served as Trump's national security adviser during the president's first term in office, but later resigned over his differences with Trump.

stories.title: India news: Government sends help to quake-hit Venezuela
stories.summary: Two air force planes carrying supplies as well as a field hospital unit are on their way. Also making headlines: Qatar has repatriated the bodies of four Indians killed in a gas explosion. DW has more.

stories.title: Four men held over child marriage in Sierra Leone appear in landmark court case
stories.summary: It relates to a 17-year-old girl - four men including her father and husband face years in jail if convicted.

stories.title: 'Help, we are trapped': Venezuela earthquake victims turn to Euronews' TikTok to beg for rescue"
stories.summary: Lack of resources and patchy telecoms have driven many Venezuelans to despair. In this context, outlets’ social networks, including Euronews, have become key platforms for appeals for help.

stories.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
stories.summary: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

stories.title: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
stories.summary: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

stories.title: EU Commission HQ forced to shut down air-conditioning amid heatwave
stories.summary: The European Commission’s headquarters was forced to shut down its air-conditioning system on Friday due to the heat wave. Staff working at the Berlaymont building received a text at midday, reading: “BERL — URGENT — Due to extreme weather conditions, forced shut down of air cooling system from floor 1 to 7 for the rest of the day.” The 13-story building is home to Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, her 26 commissioners and about 3,000 staff. Von der Leyen works on the 13th floor,

stories.title: Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached ‘alarming’ levels since aid cuts, survey finds

stories.title: Mikor lehet francia–német vagy brazil–angol a foci-vb-n? Összejöhet egy USA–Irán? Mi kell a Messi–Ronaldo-meccshez?
stories.summary: Megannyi kínzó kérdés a foci-vb-n, amikor még csak a csoportok fele zárult le. De azért mégis körvonalazódik, milyen meccsek jöhetnek a 32-es táblán, ahonnan egyenes kieséses szakaszban folytatódik a vb. Segítünk eligazodni.

stories.title: Miért olyan iszonyatosan drága a limonádé?
stories.summary: A pár száz forintos alapanyagárú italok éttermi ára egyre inkább szíven üt, ez pedig különösen elgondolkodtató olyankor, mikor hőség van. Mi kerül 2300 forintba egy limonádén?

stories.title: Nyilvános III. Károly adóbevallása, a király több mint 50 milliárd forintnyi támogatást kap az államtól egy évre
stories.summary: III. Károly király bő ötmilliárd forintnyi adót fizetett, ezzel bekerült Nagy-Britannia top 100 adófizetője közé.

stories.title: Fact-check: Tényleg jó a rák ellen az ivermectin és a fenbendazol, amikkel Schobert Norbert kezeli Rubint Rékát?
stories.summary: A fitneszguru egy praktizálástól már eltiltott orvos állításai alapján kutyáknak és macskáknak való féreghajtóval kezeli a feleségét. Megnéztük, mit mond a tudomány a két szer és a daganatkezelés kapcsolatáról.

stories.title: Majdnem 600-ra nőtt a venezuelai földrengés áldozatainak száma, több tízezer embert még mindig keresnek
stories.summary: A hivatalos bejelentőoldalakon 50 ezer eltűnt embert keresnek a rokonaik.

stories.title: Miért kéri Kapitány István, hogy ne légkondizzunk a kora esti órákban?
stories.summary: A rezsicsökkentés miatt a lakosságnak akkor sem drága az áram, ha csúcsidőben fogyaszt. Ám az ország áramrendszerét egyensúlyban tartó Mavirnak sokat segít az, aki a miniszter kérését megfogadja.

stories.title: Egymás után dőlnek meg a melegrekordok Angliában és Svájcban
stories.summary: Egyik országban sem mértek még soha júniusban olyan meleget, mint tegnap.

stories.title: Meghalt Putyin egykori erős embere
stories.summary: Szergej Ivanov volt védelmi miniszter, az elnöki hivatal helyettes vezetője, végül Putyin megbízottja. Karrierje az elnökéhez hasonlóan a leningrádi KGB-ből indult.

stories.title: Nagy tapssal fogadták el a fővárosi képviselők a plakáttilalomról szóló fővárosi javaslatot
stories.summary: A közgyűlés úgy döntött, drágulni fog a taxizás. Bemutatkozott az új rendőrfőkapitány, ahogy több fideszes és egy tiszás képviselő is.

stories.title: Távozik a Trafó igazgatója
stories.summary: Erdődi Katalin és a Fővárosi Önkormányzat közös megegyezéssel döntöttek az ügyvezetői megbízás megszüntetéséről.

stories.title: Rendkívüli állapot a Krímben, nehezen bírnak az ukrán drónokkal az orosz megszállók
stories.summary: Egyre kevésbé képes a háborút elindító Oroszország arra, hogy a megszállt területektől távol tartsa az ukrán csapásokat.

stories.title: Az uruguayi sajtó szerint uruguayi játékosok kritizálták Marcelo Bielsa módszereit, mire ő tartott egy 48 perces fejmosást
stories.summary: A válogatott kulcsjátékosaiból álló csoport állítólag nem elégedett az edzések intenzitásával és a taktikával. A spanyolok elleni meccs előtti sajtótájékoztatón viszont nem volt szó belső vitákról.

stories.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
stories.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

stories.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
stories.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

stories.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
stories.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

stories.title: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
stories.summary: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

stories.title: The 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind Albania's protests?
stories.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

stories.title: Banning social media for teens: Solution or overreaction?
stories.summary: One in seven teens in the European Union spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

stories.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
stories.summary: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

stories.title: Young people avoiding the news, Reuters report
stories.summary: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

stories.title: Germany disappoints in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

stories.title: King Charles won't live in Buckingham Palace, reveals taxes
stories.summary: Buckingham Palace has been the British monarch's official residence in London for nearly two centuries. But royal officials say King Charles and Queen Camilla won't reside there.

stories.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
stories.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

stories.title: Hormuz confusion deepens as Iran and US clash over who controls it
stories.summary: Trump said Tehran had promised no tolls or charges in the strait, hours after Iran and Oman announced a joint permit system asserting their authority over the waterway. Rubio flew to Abu Dhabi to reassure Gulf allies, as analysts warned control over Hormuz would be a major strategic victory for Iran

stories.title: At least 164 killed and 971 injured in powerful Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Residents of the capital Caracas were forced to evacuate their residences and seek shelter outdoors as the quakes, which struck less than 200 km from the capital, caused widespread damage.

stories.title: Mumbai monsoon floods streets and disrupts travel
stories.summary: Heavy monsoon rains flood Mumbai, submerging roads, disrupting traffic and forcing residents to wade through waterlogged streets in India’s financial hub.

stories.title: Trump says 'courageous' Zelenskyy is 'doing pretty well' in war with Russia
stories.summary: "You have to say he's courageous, he's got great equipment, he's got great men, he's got fighters," Trump said of Zelenskyy.

stories.title: Moment earthquake hits Venezuela, sending people fleeing in panic
stories.summary: Images showing cracked walls, collapsed ceiling panels and debris strewn across parts of Simón Bolívar International Airport highlighted the scale of damage after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026.

stories.title: Heatwave scorches Europe from London to Madrid as temperatures near 39C
stories.summary: A powerful heatwave swept across Europe, stretching from France to Spain and Belgium and pushing temperatures towards 39C, raising concerns about public safety and pressure on infrastructure.

stories.title: North Korean soldier suspected of defecting crosses demilitarised zone into South Korea
stories.summary: According to data from South Korea's Ministry of Unification, more than 34,000 North Koreans have defected to the South since its records began in 1998.

stories.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
stories.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.
